---
title: "Before Bamberg"
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: black
    slide-number: true
    transition: slide
    navigation-mode: grid
---

# Reasoning

## Change of plans and why

- Original plan
- What changed
- Why this?

## What we cover today

- My road to Bamberg
- Co-creating digital health interventions
- What next?

## Follow along (or keep for later)

![](images/image_00.png){fig-align="center"}

# Psych

## Why psych?

- Failed pre-med
- Wanted to work with people

## What I did

- Stats
- Health psychology
- Positive psychology

# Stats

## Why stats?

- Failed in getting registered as a psychotherapist
- Loved Stats

## What I did

- In-house methodology consultant
- Sample design
- Data processing, analysis & visualisation
- Statistical programming

# Psych — take two

## Why return?

- I predicted failure
- Wanted to apply stats in a psych setting

## The Marie Skłodowska-Curie Actions

- Supported my research
- Evidence based co-creation for public health
- [MSCA Funding](https://marie-sklodowska-curie-actions.ec.europa.eu)
- [Health CASCADE](https://healthcascade.eu)

# Co-creating digital health interventions

## Digital health interventions {background-image="images/image_01.jpg"}

::: footer
*Photo by <a href="https://unsplash.com/@fiens?utm_source=unsplash&utm_medium=referral&utm_content=creditCopyText">Fien Schelstraete</a> on <a href="https://unsplash.com/photos/a-man-jumping-in-the-air-Ccr9IoqzEXU?utm_source=unsplash&utm_medium=referral&utm_content=creditCopyText">Unsplash</a>*
:::

## Health behaviour change {background-image="images/image_02.jpg"}

::: footer
*Photo by <a href="https://unsplash.com/@nechirwank?utm_source=unsplash&utm_medium=referral&utm_content=creditCopyText">Nechirwan Kavian</a> on <a href="https://unsplash.com/photos/assorted-books-on-brown-wooden-shelf-T9_TDTbVo98?utm_source=unsplash&utm_medium=referral&utm_content=creditCopyText">Unsplash</a>*
:::

## Co-creation {background-image="images/image_03.jpg" background-opacity="0.6"}

::: footer
*Photo by <a href="https://unsplash.com/@domsdigitals?utm_source=unsplash&utm_medium=referral&utm_content=creditCopyText">Dominique Hicks</a> on <a href="https://unsplash.com/photos/a-dirt-road-in-front-of-a-red-building-yW9ko_3OIyA?utm_source=unsplash&utm_medium=referral&utm_content=creditCopyText">Unsplash</a>*
:::

## The solution

- Incorporate end users and stakeholders
- A flexible behaviour change framework
- Make co-creation more trustworthy

## The behaviour change framework {background-image="images/image_04.png" background-opacity="0.6"}

::: footer
*Photo by <a href="https://unsplash.com/@domsdigitals?utm_source=unsplash&utm_medium=referral&utm_content=creditCopyText">Dominique Hicks</a> on <a href="https://unsplash.com/photos/a-dirt-road-in-front-of-a-red-building-yW9ko_3OIyA?utm_source=unsplash&utm_medium=referral&utm_content=creditCopyText">Unsplash</a>*
:::

## The behaviour change framework

- [Behaviour Change Wheel](https://www.behaviourchangewheel.com)
- [Training](http://www.bct-taxonomy.com)
- [Tools & Techniques](https://www.ucl.ac.uk/brain-sciences/behaviour-change/resources/advancing-behavioural-science)

## Making co-creation more trustworthy {background-image="images/image_06.png" background-opacity="0.6"}

::: footer
*Photo by <a href="https://unsplash.com/@pawelskor?utm_source=unsplash&utm_medium=referral&utm_content=creditCopyText">Paul Skorupskas</a> on <a href="https://unsplash.com/photos/person-holding-camera-lens-7KLa-xLbSXA?utm_source=unsplash&utm_medium=referral&utm_content=creditCopyText">Unsplash</a>*
:::

## Making co-creation more trustworthy

- [Defining co-creation](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0033350625003683?__cf_chl_f_tk=JZ63rdp9A8OY79qk7QNLAP86v1UZsFlQvDKdpGqnjX4-1782803275-1.0.1.1-_oM93EpJ5K4JOgVdj1ybVW71pKe8KvHohhr74waGMoM)
- [Co-creation methods selector](https://ccmethodsselector.lovable.app)
- [Evaluating your co-creation process](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40449476/)

## My PhD

- How to design digital behaviour change interventions?
- How to build digital behaviour change interventions?
- How to evaluate digital behaviour change interventions?

## Designing digital behaviour change interventions

- Who -> Teams that developed digital health tools
- What -> Identifying facilitators and challenges to the co-creation process
- How -> Case study methodology

## Designing digital behaviour change interventions

- Who -> Students at an international university in Germany
- What -> Exploring needs and putting together a design specification for a digital behaviour change intervention
- How -> Workshops

## Building digital behaviour change interventions

- Who -> Users (expectant mothers) of a web-app that was developed to facilitate doctor-patient communication
- What -> Monitor the build to see if iterations (based on end user and stakeholder) feedback were being received more positively
- How -> Sentiment analysis + User engagement measures of version 1 vs 2

## Evaluating digital behaviour change interventions

- Who -> Users (heart-event patients) of a native-app that was developed to facilitate cardiovascular rehabilitation
- What -> Compare different missingness strategies with respect to bias and variance of estimates
- How -> Synthetic data + Simulation

## Evaluating digital behaviour change interventions

- Who -> Clinic that used virtual training to help expectant mothers work more effectively with midwives and doctors
- What -> Compare different models in predicting costs of patient care
- How -> Training and Test split

# The Now

## What drives me? {background-image="images/image_05.png" background-opacity="0.6"}

## Looking forward

- Cumulative health behaviour change research
- Understanding & reporting on uncertainty
- Making our work more accessible

## What I want to learn?

- Bayesian approach
- Experimental research
- Art & Science

## Where do our paths cross?

- Something you can teach me
- Something I can teach you
- Something we can work together on
